body.notes: Observed 2026-04-19 during T-swadl-concepts-in-graph. MCP channel_send returns 'Remote end closed connection without response' on every attempt; direct POST to http://localhost:8082/api/cc_send works fine. Symptom: CC has to fall back to curl. Hypothesis: MCP server (igor_mcp.py) may be targeting the HTTPS port 8080 with stale/no-verify SSL, or UC's channel_send endpoint was never wired to match MCP's expectation. Verify by grepping igor_mcp.py for channel_send path + matching UC routes. Fix: align the transport, either make MCP hit 8082 plain-HTTP or add the missing HTTPS route to UC.
body.title: mcp__igor__channel_send fails with 'Remote end closed connection without response'
body.result: One-line fix in igor_mcp.py: CC_SEND_URL default changed from port 8081 to 8082 (UC's plain-HTTP companion). Smoke-tested the exact code path: {'status': 'ok'}. Shipped cadb4dd3. MCP channel_send will work after Akien's Claude Code session reloads the MCP server (next CC restart / claude --resume).
